0x4013e0.name: DialogFunc
0x4013e0.repeatable: DialogFunc: minimal dialog callback — WM_CLOSE (0x10) ends with 0, WM_INITDIALOG (272) returns 1, WM_COMMAND OK (10000) ends with 1. Used by ShowConfigDialog to show the generic 'configuration' dialog (About box?).

0x401430.name: ShowConfigDialog
0x401430.repeatable: ShowConfigDialog: wraps DialogBoxParamA with DialogFunc and template in TemplateName. Invoked from HandleMenuCommand case 0xB5E (Help->About/Settings menu item).

0x401a40.name: UpdateMenuState
0x401a40.repeatable: UpdateMenuState: toggles enable/check state for menu items BC5 (Save), BC4 (SaveAs), CBC, CB2 (TestPlay) based on g_projectLoaded. Also syncs radio-check state for round-time/difficulty/floating-window/reaction-list/preview-zoom/auto-hitjunction toggles. Re-renders all 35 shortcut menu strings by composing '<label>\tCtrl+Shift+Alt+<VK_STR>' for menu IDs 3300..3334.

0x401c50.name: MainWndProc
0x401c50.repeatable: Main editor window proc. Handles WM_CREATE (1): creates tooltip + status bar + posts WM_COMMAND 0xA (init project). WM_DESTROY (2): cleanup. WM_SIZE (5): resize status bar + active tab. WM_CLOSE (0x10): prompt save-if-dirty then DefWndProc. WM_COMMAND (0x111): routes to HandleMenuCommand; special-cases wParam==10 (init project after open) and wParam==10020 (TCN_SELCHANGE).

0x402460.name: HandleKeyPress
0x402460.repeatable: Keyboard shortcut router. Called from WinMain message loop for WM_KEYDOWN. Modifier bits OR'd into slot value: 0x10000=Ctrl, 0x20000=Shift, 0x40000=Alt. Matches against 35-entry g_keyboardShortcutTable[i]; dispatches to ActionEditorMain (cells 0-17), tab nav (18-21), sub-tab nav (22-27), or HandleMenuCommand (28-34: New/Open/SaveAs/Save/CompileGame/TestPlay/Quit). VK_TAB (0x09) traps Tab/Shift+Tab to cycle focus through visible editor tab panes.

0x403540.name: AppendFilterString
0x403540.repeatable: Strcpy-append: like AppendPathComponent but without inserting a backslash separator. Used to glue the ' /T' / ' /F' command-line flags onto the KGT2nd_GAME.exe path.

0x403650.name: TextInputDialog
0x403650.repeatable: TextInputDialog: modal text-input dialog proc. Reads initial title/prompt from lpString (first 128 bytes = title, next 128 bytes = prompt). Returns entered string in lpString. IDOK=10000, IDCANCEL=10001 or 2. Used by RenameHitJunction/InsertHitJunction/RenameCommonImage/RenameCharacterSlot/etc.

0x404810.name: StopAndFreeWavPlayback
0x404810.repeatable: Stops any currently-playing WAV via sndPlaySoundA(NULL, 2) and frees the global pszWavSoundBuffer.

0x404860.name: DispatchSoundPlayback
0x404860.repeatable: Dispatches a sound-entry (KgtSoundSlotEntry*) by its soundType low nibble: 0=stop/cleanup, 1=WAV via sndPlaySoundA, 2=MIDI via PlayMidiFromSoundEntry, 3=CD-audio via InitializeCDAudio.

0x4061b0.name: BlitPalettedSprite
0x4061b0.repeatable:
BlitPalettedSprite: blits an 8-bit paletted sprite to the 16bpp (RGB555) framebuffer at ppvBits.
Args:
  pixels  = source 8bpp pixel data (byte per pixel, palette indices)
  palette = 256-entry palette. Each entry is 32-bit ARGB-like where the renderer extracts RGB in 5-5-5 bits from positions (>>19 red, >>11 green, >>3 blue) each masked by 0x1F.
           (This layout matches KgtColorBgra stored in the editor's shared-palette arrays: blue << 3, green << 11, red << 19 combined.)
  dstX, dstY     = destination offset in framebuffer (in pixels; global framebuffer is at g_framebufferWidth x g_framebufferHeight)
  srcW, srcH     = sprite dimensions
  srcX, srcY     = source-rect offset inside pixels
  flags          = bit 0..2 blend mode (case 0=opaque, 1=50/50 alpha blend (RGB555 &~0xC210 sum-of-halves), 2=additive saturating, 3=subtractive saturating, 4=alpha-blend using rOffset=alphaR/gOffset=alphaG/bOffset=alphaB as 5-bit coefficients, 7=raw copy).
                   bit 0x20000000 = 'force single scanline stride' (uses global dword_7D7A38 pitch)
                   bit 0x40000000 = 'vertical flip'; sign bit (0x80000000) = horizontal flip
  rOffset/gOffset/bOffset: additive color offsets added to palette entry's R/G/B BEFORE lookup (used for palette brightness cycling; clamped to 0..31). In case 4 these are repurposed as blend alphas.

Local v60[256] is the per-sprite palette LUT: each palette entry's modified RGB packed as 15-bit RGB555 (value << R:10 | G:5 | B:0, bit 0x8000 unused). Index 0 reserved for 'transparent' (stays 0). If pixel is transparent (value==0) the output is skipped in all blend modes.

Key internal facts:
  v60[i] = r5*32*32 + g5*32 + b5 where r5,g5,b5 are palette RGB channels clamped to 0..31 after adding rOffset/gOffset/bOffset offsets; if resulting RGB is all-zero, set to 1 to avoid collision with transparent sentinel.
  Output pixel format is 16bpp RGB555 (little endian). Blend ops match the standard Genesis/SNES-style 'sum-of-halves' RGB555 blend.

0x4068d0.name: RemapPaletteIndices
0x4068d0.repeatable:
RemapPaletteIndices: de-duplicates the RGBQUAD palette of the BMP-editor's DIB (stru_7D7600 a.k.a. g_bmpEditorBMI).
For each of the imported BMP's colorCount palette entries, it searches the accumulated unique colors and builds a remap table v14[oldIdx] -> newIdx. Duplicates collapse onto the first occurrence. Remaining slots (from the new unique count up to 256) are zeroed out. Finally, the paletted pixel buffer g_bmpEditorPixels is rewritten in-place through v14[], compacting duplicates.

0x406a90.name: InitializeRenderContext
0x406a90.repeatable: InitializeRenderContext: zero out the global offscreen DC and the bmp-editor DIB memory-DC at startup.

0x4073d0.name: RegisterEditorWindowClasses
0x4073d0.repeatable: Registers all editor WndProc classes. Pre-fills 62 x 144-byte class buffers starting at g_windowClassBuffers (0x7D5200) with auto-generated names 'kgt2nd N'. Also registers 8 special classes with static names 'kgt2kCounterInput', 'kgt2kCounterPM5Input', 'kgt2kCounterInput2', 'kgt2kCounterPM5Input2', 'kgt2kWazaNbInput', 'kgt2kWazaNbInput_tab', 'kgt2kHitJunctionInput' (via off_4451F4 / 'kgt2kCommandInput' via lpClassName).

0x407b40.name: DeactivateEditorTab
0x407b40.repeatable: DeactivateEditorTab(classBufAddr): hides an editor tab. If the tab's classIdx is in range 42..45 (BMP editor / palette editor / animation preview / anim tree — the popup ones), clears the visible flag, un-checks the rebar toolbar button (ID = classIdx+40959), and sends WM_CLOSE to the editor pane.

0x407bb0.name: CreateEditorTabWindow
0x407bb0.repeatable: CreateEditorTabWindow(lpClassName, hParent): creates a child editor pane window using the given pre-built class buffer. Switches style + dwExStyle by the class's kgt2nd index (stored at offset +34 of class buffer). Indices 35-38 use WS_CAPTION-like popup style when g_floatingWindowMode is enabled. Sets the newly-created HWND into class_buf[+32].

0x407d50.name: CreateEditorChildPanel
0x407d50.repeatable: CreateEditorChildPanel(lpClassName, hParent): simpler variant that creates a WS_CHILD+WS_VISIBLE child. Stores HWND into class_buf[+32] and parent HWND into class_buf[+33].

0x407e10.name: ToolbarRebarWndProc
0x407e10.repeatable: kgt2nd class index 41 (g_windowClassBuffers[41]) = 0x7D6910. Toolbar rebar wrapper.

0x408dd0.name: EditorTabContainerWndProc
0x408dd0.repeatable: kgt2nd class index 31 (g_windowClassBuffers[31]) = 0x7D6370. Main editor tab container.

0x40a600.name: InsertHitJunction
0x40a600.repeatable:
InsertHitJunction(hWndParent, afterIdx) -- creates a new reaction entry.
Checks free slot count, auto-generates a candidate name ('Attack %4d'), prompts user for final name (must be <=31 chars, unique), then:
 - shifts reactionItems[afterIdx..199] right by one
 - writes the new name into reactionItems[afterIdx].reactionName
 - for every populated character, shifts scripts[488] 4-byte-per-reaction hurt-bind records right
 - for every character's type-23 script-items, calls AdjustByteIndexAfterInsert on bytes[0,2,4,6,8,10] so existing reaction-refs >= afterIdx are renumbered
 - RefreshHitJunctionList.

0x40aa70.name: SwapHitJunctions
0x40aa70.repeatable:
SwapHitJunctions(a1, hMem, a3) -- swaps reaction slots hMem and a3 across global and per-character data.
For a reorder drag in the reaction list it:
 1. swaps 36 bytes between g_kgtGameSystemData.reactionItems[hMem] and [a3]
 2. for every populated character (g_characterSlots[i]): swaps 4-byte hurt-bind record at scripts[488].scriptName[4*idx+6]..[4*idx+9] = { uint16 scriptId; uint16 effectObjectId }
 3. for every type-23 ([R] Reaction) script-item in each character: swaps single-byte reaction-IDs at bytes[0], [2], [4], [6], [8], [10] -- these are the 6 junction slots (stand-hit/crouch-hit/air-hit/stand-guard/crouch-guard/air-guard; script-item bytes: +1,+3,+5,+7,+9,+11 counting the leading type byte).

0x40acf0.name: HitJunctionListWndProc
0x40acf0.repeatable:
WndProc for the global 200-slot reaction-item list (class byte_7D65B0).
Displays g_kgtGameSystemData.reactionItems[0..199].reactionName in a vertical 16-px-high list with scrollbar.
LMB-drag reorders via SwapHitJunctions (updates per-character scripts[488] hurt-bind packed table AND swaps byte-operands inside every type-23 [R] script item).
RMB opens popup menu: 0xABE1=InsertHitJunction, 0xABE2=RenameHitJunction, 0xABE3=DeleteHitJunction.
Double-click (WM_LBUTTONDBLCLK 0x203) fires 0xABE2 on populated slot, 0xABE1 on empty.
Selected index stored in g_currentReactionIdx (1-based).

0x40b520.name: ReactionIsHurtToggleWndProc
0x40b520.repeatable:
WndProc for the 'Doing' toggle under the reaction list (class unk_7D6520).
Single checkbox bound to id 100 (aDoing_0='Doing').
When toggled, sets bit 0 of g_kgtGameSystemData.reactionItems[g_currentReactionIdx].isHurtAction (KgtReactionItem.isHurtAction field).
This is the 'Doing' selection referenced in the [R] Reaction codeblock docs: 'If a move is Guarded it won't deal damage unless that hit junction has the Doing selection enabled.'

0x40de60.name: HitAttributeFlagsWndProc
0x40de60.repeatable:
WndProc for the FA/FD Hit-Attribute Flags widget (class lpClassName).
A custom 160x16 BitBlt'd control that packs a 16-bit parameter word with this layout (from [FA]/[FD] codeblock docs):
  bits  0..3  (4b): M.Number   (hitbox/hurtbox identifier 0..13; doc says 0..19 but widget caps at 13) -- subcase v24=1
  bit   4        : flag bit 0  (Cancel)                        -- v24=2 XOR mask 1<<4
  bit   5        : flag bit 1  (No Detection)                  -- v24=3 XOR mask 1<<5
  bit   6        : flag bit 2  (Cont. Hits)                    -- v24=4 XOR mask 1<<6
  bit   7        : flag bit 3  (No Sky Decision)               -- v24=5 XOR mask 1<<7
  bit   8        : flag bit 4  (Guard Fail)                    -- v24=6 XOR mask 1<<8
  bit   9        : flag bit 5  (While Guard)                   -- v24=7 XOR mask 1<<9
  bits 14..15 (2b): While-Receiving / Shave 2-bit selector     -- v24=8,9
WM_LBUTTONDOWN=+1, WM_RBUTTONDOWN=-1 on the identifier+selector; toggles flag-bit for checkboxes.
Sends WM_COMMAND to parent with wParam=(propOffset[+0 or +1]), lParam=new value.

0x40ec40.name: CommandListWndProc
0x40ec40.repeatable: CommandListWndProc: scrollable list of KgtCommandInput entries. Each row 82 bytes at slot[1]+11110+82*i

0x40f450.name: CommandEditPanelWndProc
0x40f450.repeatable: CommandEditPanelWndProc: edits KgtCommandInput at index g_currentCommandIdx

0x40fb70.name: ThrowReactionEditPanelWndProc
0x40fb70.repeatable:
WndProc for the Throw Reaction Edit Panel (class unk_7D5A70).
Edits per-character throw-animation parameters for the currently-selected throw reaction (g_currentThrowReactionIdx) of character g_currentEditorSlot.
Fields at &g_characterSlots[slot][1].scripts[508].scriptName[6*idx + 26]:
  +0 (2 bytes): picNo   (KgtThrowActionInfo.picNo, 0..0x1FFF)
  +2 (2 bytes): offsetX (-999..+999)
  +4 (2 bytes): offsetY (-999..+999)
I.e. per-character 6-byte-per-throw-reaction table of 200 entries (1200 bytes total).
The top paint handler tallies free throw-reaction slots using g_kgtGameSystemData.throwReactions (200 × 32 bytes).

0x410ed0.name: SkillSelectListWndProc
0x410ed0.repeatable:
WndProc for the Skill/Command Select popup list (class byte_7D73C0).
Iterates 100 × 82-byte character command entries starting at g_characterSlots[slot][1].scripts[275].scriptName and displays them as a scrolling list.
On click/Enter, stores the selected index (g_skillPickerSelectedIdx a.k.a. word_44E66E) into *g_skillPickerResultPtr (a 2-byte field in the caller's data) and calls MarkSlotModified.
DOES NOT operate on reaction items -- this is the generic skill/command picker used when binding scripts.
Typical flow: HitJunctionListPanelWndProc sets g_skillPickerResultPtr to &char_slot.scripts[488].scriptName[4*reactionIdx+6|8], then CreateSkillListPopup spawns the popup which contains this list.

0x412800.name: CpuListPanelWndProc
0x412800.repeatable: CpuListPanelWndProc: scrollable list of KgtCpuEntry. Each row 111 bytes at slot[1]+10+111*i

0x4132f0.name: CpuPropertiesPanelWndProc
0x4132f0.repeatable: CpuPropertiesPanelWndProc: edits KgtCpuEntry at index g_currentCpuIdx

0x414940.name: ActionEditorMainWndProc
0x414940.repeatable:
ActionEditorMainWndProc @ 0x414940

Top-level parent window for the action (script/skill) editor. Hosts:
  * AnimationTreePanel on the left (256 wide)
  * Action editor tabs (stats/properties) on the right
  * Numeric spinner controls at top: 'script select' (g_selectedScriptIdx)
    and 'step select' (g_selectedCellIdx)
  * Status bar at bottom

Layout in WM_CREATE:
  g_animTreeTabEntry (dword_7D5670) = left pane (0, 72, 256, h-100)
  dword_7D5700 = right pane (256, 18, w-256, h-18)
  dword_7D55E0 = toolbar (0, 0, w, 18)
  dword_7D6990 = status bar (0, h-26, 256, 26)

WM_PAINT draws the current cell's opcode label + icon at (10..40, 50..70)
using off_445078[opcode] (the per-opcode label string table) and the icon
tile at 16*opcode in hdcSrc.

WM_COMMAND 1000: jump to (script, cell) via NavigateToEntryAndCell after
resetting the two sentinel values.
WM_COMMAND 10:   refresh the spinner controls with current values.

0x414db0.name: AnimationTreePanelWndProc
0x414db0.repeatable:
AnimationTreePanelWndProc @ 0x414DB0

The 'tree' view on the LEFT side of the action editor — despite the name it's
actually a flat hierarchical list of all (script, cell) pairs for the current
project slot, one row per cell. Left column = script name, right area = cell
icons (one 16x16 per cell).

State globals:
  g_selectedScriptIdx / g_selectedCellIdx = current selection
  qword_450270 (hi/lo) = scroll offset
  dword_4501C0/C4 = scroll maxima
  g_animTreeLeftButtonDown / g_animTreeRightButtonDown = drag state
  g_animTreeDraggedRowIdx = row being drag-swapped with SwapSkillEntries
  g_animTreeTimerId = auto-scroll timer (100 ms)

Key behaviors:
  * Left-drag within the tree swaps adjacent skill entries (SwapSkillEntries).
  * Right-click opens a context menu (menu resource MenuTree). Subindex chosen
    based on g_currentEditorMode (0,2,3 -> scripts submenu; 1 -> skills submenu)
    and whether the right-clicked row is an empty script.
  * Double-click (0x203 WM_LBUTTONDBLCLK) posts 0x4E84 (create new) or 0x4E98
    (insert cell) to the parent.
  * WM_COMMAND 12345: scroll the tree to ensure g_selectedScriptIdx is visible.
  * WM_COMMAND 10100: recount visible rows and update scroll maxima.

Draws using GDI double-buffering (CompatibleDC). Each row:
  * script-name column filled with system color 5 (window) or 13 (highlight)
  * cell icons blitted from hdcSrc using opcode byte as tile index
  * selected row gets a top/bottom highlight line in window background color

0x4194e0.name: CodeblockPropertyEditorWndProc
0x4194e0.repeatable: Per-opcode property editor WndProc. Dispatches to different sub-panels based on the type byte of the currently-selected KgtScriptItem (16-byte slot in scriptItems[] of the active character/stage slot). Used for [I]/[M]/[FA]/[FD]/[R]/[O]/[S]/[V]/[COLOR]/[AI] etc.

0x421290.name: HitJunctionEditorPanelWndProc
0x421290.repeatable:
WndProc for the Hit-Junction Editor tab container (class unk_7D5C20).
Hosts column headers 'Hit Junction' (x=5), 'Alloment' (x=205), 'Spark' (x=405) and embeds HitJunctionListPanelWndProc (class byte_7D5B90) as the editable body.

0x4214b0.name: HitJunctionListPanelWndProc
0x4214b0.repeatable:
WndProc for the per-character Hit-Junction Binding Panel (class byte_7D5B90).
Shows a 3-column table of all 200 reactions for character g_currentEditorSlot:
  col 1 (x=5..199):    reactionName (from g_kgtGameSystemData.reactionItems[i].reactionName)
  col 2 (x=205..399):  name of the hurt-animation skill (lookup into scripts[] via 1-byte script index at scripts[488].scriptName[4*i+6])
  col 3 (x=405..):     name of the hit-spark/effect skill (scripts[488].scriptName[4*i+8])
Each reaction i owns a 4-byte record at scripts[488].scriptName[4*i+6..4*i+9] = { uint16 scriptId; uint16 effectObjectId; } -- matches HurtBindInfo in 2dfm/KgtPlayer.hpp.
LMB (WM_LBUTTONDOWN): if x<200 ignored; 200<=x<400 selects col2 and CreateSkillListPopup on scripts[488].scriptName[4*i+6]; x>=400 selects col3 and opens picker on +8 field.

0x421f90.name: StoryModeSettingsPanelWndProc
0x421f90.repeatable: StoryModeSettingsPanelWndProc: gates whether story mode is enabled for this character (via playerSelectableInfos[slotIdx] & 1)

0x4223b0.name: StoryEventListPanelWndProc
0x4223b0.repeatable: StoryEventListPanelWndProc: scrollable list of KgtStoryEntry (206 bytes each). 100 max.

0x425e20.name: ImportBgFromNamedFile
0x425e20.repeatable:
Import-bg helper — loads .stage file into stage slot stageIdx.

Backs up lpString (256 bytes) → copies the caller-supplied KgtNameInfo into g_kgtGameSystemData.stageNames[stageIdx] → calls LoadStageFile(stageIdx) (which uses the stageNames[] entry to build the path). On success copies the backed-up name back into both g_stageSlots[stageIdx]->header.name and g_kgtGameSystemData.stageNames[stageIdx].

0x425eb0.name: ImportBgFromFileDialog
0x425eb0.repeatable: Import-bg dialog — ShowOpenFileDialog('*.stage'), parses base name from path (strip extension + directory), uniquifies via TextInputDialog if duplicate/invalid, opens slot a2 by memmoving stages [a2..48] down one slot (g_stageSlots[], g_stageSlotFlags[], stageNames[]) to make room, zero-inits the new slot, then calls ImportBgFromNamedFile to actually load the .stage data.

0x427860.name: ImportDemoFromNamedFile
0x427860.repeatable: Import-demo helper — identical shape to ImportBgFromNamedFile but for demos: copies KgtNameInfo into g_kgtGameSystemData.demoNames[demoIdx], calls LoadDemoFile(demoIdx), restores the backed-up name.

0x4278f0.name: ImportDemoFromFileDialog
0x4278f0.repeatable: Import-demo dialog — ShowOpenFileDialog('*.demo'), strip/validate base name, uniquify via TextInputDialog, memmove [a2..98] down one demo slot, zero-init, ImportDemoFromNamedFile to load. After load: fixup demoConfig.titleDemoId[8] and stage-slot scripts via AdjustWordIndexAfterInsert.

0x42cfe0.name: PaletteRemapDlgProc
0x42cfe0.repeatable: PaletteRemapDlgProc: loops over the 8192-entry pictureHeaders array (stride 20 = sizeof KgtPictureSlotEntry), for each sprite: decompress (if compressed via DecompressSpriteData), remap every pixel through g_paletteRemapTable[256], re-store the result. Private-palette sprites skip the first 1024 bytes (the palette prefix). Progressbar (dword_451674) is ticked per-sprite.

0x42d1c0.name: ShowPaletteRemapDialog
0x42d1c0.repeatable: ShowPaletteRemapDialog: launches the 'Pal Remap' progress dialog (id A101, handler PaletteRemapDlgProc). The dialog's WM_INITDIALOG (272) sets up a progressbar; WM_CLOSE-ish (15) iterates g_projectSlot.pictureHeaders[8192], and for every picture rewrites its 8bpp pixel data by applying g_paletteRemapTable[] in place, handling both compressed (has non-zero .size field) and uncompressed sprites plus private-palette offset of 1024 bytes.

0x42d230.name: SortPaletteDarkToLight
0x42d230.repeatable: SortPaletteDarkToLight: compute brightness key = (r>>3)+(g>>3)+(b>>3) for each of the 256 colors in the currently-selected shared palette; active (alpha!=0) slots get +1, inactive zero-RGB slots get -1 so they sort last. Selection sort: when swapping, mirror the swap across ALL 8 shared palettes (so all 8 remain aligned index-wise). Also fill g_paletteRemapTable[oldIdx] = newIdx and call ShowPaletteRemapDialog which rewrites every sprite's pixel data.

0x42d3e0.name: FindAndSwapPaletteColor
0x42d3e0.repeatable: FindAndSwapPaletteColor(r5, g5, b5, &cursor, backMap): scan from cursor upward; find first active palette entry matching the 5-bit (r,g,b) quantized RGB in the currently-selected shared palette. If found, swap it with position *cursor across ALL 8 shared palettes (loop j=0..2112 step 264 covers the 8 palettes offset by 1056/4 dwords each), update backMap (sort-stable index), increment *cursor.

0x42d530.name: SortPaletteByChannel
0x42d530.repeatable: SortPaletteByChannel: sort colors by *dominant channel* into a specific order. For each 5-bit R value (31..0) fill positions where max(R,G,B)==R; then by G-dominance, then B-dominance. Calls FindAndSwapPaletteColor to do the in-place reordering of all 8 shared palettes simultaneously.

0x42d630.name: ColorCountInputDlgProc
0x42d630.repeatable: ColorCountInputDlgProc: 'Enter new color count (1-N)' dialog for ReducePaletteColors. Writes result to g_paletteReduceTargetCount.

0x42d780.name: ReducePaletteColors
0x42d780.repeatable: ReducePaletteColors: iteratively merges the two most-similar active palette entries until the active-count drops to g_paletteReduceTargetCount (entered via ColorCountInputDlg). Similarity metric = sum-of-3x quantized channel sums / 10 + squared-distance in 8-bit RGB space. Found pair: overwrite slot v6 with averaged-and-quantized color (&0xF8 per channel), zero out slot v22 across all 8 shared palettes, update g_paletteRemapTable[v22] = v6 (and any previously-remapped entries). At the end calls ShowPaletteRemapDialog to rewrite sprite pixels with the new indices.

0x42db30.name: PaletteEditorWndProc
0x42db30.repeatable:
PaletteEditorWndProc: the interactive palette-editor window.
Visualization: paints the 16x16 grid of 256 colors from g_projectSlot.sharedPalettes[g_selectedPaletteIdx] (byte offset 1252624 within the slot). Alpha byte (index+3) indicates the slot is 'active' (1) or empty (0); empty slots show a white dot.
Mouse: WM_LBUTTONDBLCLK (Msg 515) calls ChooseColor on the clicked palette entry; if the new color duplicates an existing one, prompts to reuse the duplicate.
WM_MOUSEMOVE updates (g_paletteEditorCursorX/Y) and repaints; status bar shows 'pal-R:G:B'.
Toolbar commands:
  0xB3B1..0xB3B8: select one of 8 shared palettes
  0xB3BB: SortPaletteDarkToLight
  0xB3BC: SortPaletteByChannel
  0xB3C4: ReducePaletteColors

0x42ea80.name: AnimationPreviewWndProc
0x42ea80.repeatable:
AnimationPreviewWndProc @ 0x42EA80

The 'play' window that shows the currently selected skill animating in real
time. Hosts a child tool-bar (rebar) with buttons to toggle hurtboxes
(A7F9), hitboxes (A7FA), sound (A7FB), and half-resolution (A7FC).

The per-frame tick happens in UpdateAnimationFrame @ 0x436AF0 — NOT here. This
WndProc only handles:
  * WM_CREATE:  SetupAnimationPreview() sets up entity objects in the object
                pool for the currently selected script.
  * WM_CLOSE/WM_DESTROY (0x2/0x10): cleanup + save window rect.
  * WM_PAINT:   CreateRenderSurfaceWithSize, FillFramebufferRect (bg),
                FillFramebufferRect for grid lines, RenderAllGameObjects
                (walks the object pool + calls ExecuteAnimationScript per tick),
                then BitBlt/StretchBlt to the window DC.
  * WM_LBUTTONDOWN / MOUSEMOVE / LBUTTONUP: drag to pan the preview origin
    (dword_7D51C0, dword_7D51C4) with cursor confinement.
  * WM_COMMAND A7F9..A7FC: toggle display flags and re-set up preview.

g_currentEditorMode determines canvas size (SCRIPT mode: 640x480; SKILL/
PLAYER mode: 640 wide + GetSystemMetrics padding).

dword_62ED5C (char half-res) and dword_62ED60 (non-char half-res) cause the
render to be 2x framebuffer and StretchBlt'd down.

0x42ffb0.name: NumericInputDlgProc
0x42ffb0.repeatable: NumericInputDlgProc: modal numeric-input dialog. Like TextInputDialog but atoi's the result into g_numericInputResult. Used by NumericSpinControlWndProc / NumericDisplayControlWndProc for direct numeric entry.

0x4314a0.name: SkillListPanelWndProc
0x4314a0.repeatable:
SkillListPanelWndProc @ 0x4314A0

The skill-list panel that shows all named scripts (skills) for the current character/slot.
Each row is 16 px tall. Items enumerated by walking g_activeSlotPtr->scripts[1024],
filtering to entries with non-empty scriptName.

State globals:
  g_skillPickerSelectedIdx (WORD) = currently selected skill row
  qword_451728 = scroll offset (hi=y-scroll, lo=x-scroll)
  dword_451730/4 = scroll maxima (width/height in cells)
  g_skillListPopupHostHwnd = parent window to notify on selection

Message map:
  WM_CREATE: init scroll position from g_skillPickerSelectedIdx
  WM_PAINT (0xF): enumerate scripts, draw each row; highlight selected
  WM_COMMAND 0x2774: recount valid scripts and update scrollbars
  WM_LBUTTONDOWN: commit selection via *g_skillPickerResultPtr = selected
  WM_KEYDOWN VK_ESCAPE / WM_COMMAND 6,0: cancel popup

IDA offsets decoded:
  +272 = scripts[i].scriptName
  +304 = scripts[i].scriptIndex (start of items)
  +343 = scripts[i+1].scriptIndex (= end of items for script i)
  +306 = scripts[i].gap
  +307 = scripts[i].flags (ScriptSpecialFlag)
  The +152 compares in the decompile are register-reuse artifacts; actual
  compare is scripts[i].scriptIndex == scripts[i+1].scriptIndex (empty-script test).

0x431cc0.name: SkillCellGridPanelWndProc
0x431cc0.repeatable:
SkillCellGridPanelWndProc @ 0x431CC0

Horizontal codeblock-cell timeline for the 'skill picker' pop-up. Each selected
skill displays its cells (scriptItems from scripts[i].scriptIndex to
scripts[i+1].scriptIndex - 1) as a row of 16x16 icons starting 16*12=192 px
into each row, left of which is the 32-byte script name.

State globals:
  g_skillPickerSelectedIdx (WORD)   = which script (row)
  g_skillCellGridPopupCellIdx (BYTE) = which cell within that script
  g_skillPickerOriginalValue         = value on popup open (for 'original' highlight)
  g_skillCellGridPopupOrigCellIdx    = cell value on popup open
  g_skillPickerResultPtr (WORD*)     = caller's result slot (filled on click)
  g_skillCellGridResultCellIdxPtr    = caller's cell-idx result slot
  qword_451750 = scroll offset
  dword_451740/4 = scroll maxima

Each cell's icon index comes from: opcode byte at slot+40208+16*itemIdx,
passed through the byte-to-icon table to produce a source rect in hdcSrc
(16 * icon_idx, 16) in the tileset.

The arrow line connects original cell (dword_601B80 in selected script) to
the currently-hovered cell — a visual indicator of what the pop-up is about
to commit.

0x432df0.name: SkillNameLabelWndProc
0x432df0.repeatable:
SkillNameLabelWndProc @ 0x432DF0

One-line label control shown next to each codeblock-operand slot in the
property editor. Displays the skill name referenced by the operand.

Uses window properties (GetPropA):
  'SKILL_DESC_STRUCT' (asc_44CF5C) -> byte[] state blob:
    byte 0   : flags (bit 0 = grid-popup mode; bit 10 = disabled)
    byte 1   : render flags (bit 2 = 'unavailable' style)
    byte 2,4 : popup origin offsets
    byte 5   : pressed flag
    byte 6   : cell-idx operand ptr (for grid popup)
    byte 10  : script-idx operand ptr (for list popup)
    byte 14  : WM_COMMAND id to post to parent on completion

WM_LBUTTONDOWN (0x201):
  * flag bit 0 set -> ShowSkillCellGridPopup
  * else           -> ShowSkillListPopup

WM_COMMAND 10 (0xA): skill-idx committed by SkillListPopup (ho = new idx)
WM_COMMAND 11 (0xB): cell-idx committed by SkillCellGridPopup
WM_COMMAND 20 (0x14): popup cancelled; notify parent with id in PropA[14]

0x4335d0.name: RenameSoundEntry
0x4335d0.repeatable:
Sound-slot CRUD — RENAME entry at soundIdx.

Prompts TextInputDialog for a new name (up to 31 chars). Rejects duplicates unless the duplicate IS soundIdx itself (no-op). Copies up to sizeof(KgtSoundSlotEntry.name) into slot.soundHeaders[soundIdx].name, MarkSlotModified, ResetEditorViewState.

0x4336e0.name: AddSoundEntry
0x4336e0.repeatable:
Sound-slot CRUD — INSERT new sound at index soundIdx.

Driven by the sounds panel; type 1=WAV (file dialog → .wav), 2=MIDI (file dialog → .mid), 3=CD-track (synthesised name 'CDtrack N').

Flow:
  - Abort if slot 255 is occupied (list full).
  - For types 1/2, ShowOpenFileDialog, strip path → name in lpString+0x80.
  - If overwriteExisting!=0: free the existing GlobalAlloc at slot[soundIdx] and reuse the slot.
  - Else: uniquify the proposed name (loops 'dddddddd%d' until unique), DialogBoxParam(TextInputDialog) to let user edit it, then memmove tail to open a hole at soundIdx, fill entry metadata.
  - For WAV/MIDI (type<=2): CreateFileA → GlobalAlloc(size) → GlobalLock → ReadFile → GlobalUnlock → stores pointer in slot[soundIdx].data and size in slot[soundIdx].size.
  - For CD-track (type=3): just writes slot.track = 1 (no data load).
  - When !overwriteExisting: walks all 65536 scriptItems; for each PLAY_SOUND opcode (type byte 3) calls AdjustWordIndexAfterInsert to bump refs to sounds at or above soundIdx. Also updates the stage/demo slot's script if the editor is in stage/demo mode.

Return: 0 = OK, -1 = any error.  MarkSlotModified + ResetEditorViewState on success.

0x433bb0.name: DeleteSoundEntry
0x433bb0.repeatable:
Sound-slot CRUD — DELETE entry at soundIdx.

Flow:
  - Refuse to delete slot 0 (reserved silence/none).
  - Confirm with MessageBox ('Delete %s from game file?'), cancel on IDNO.
  - Walks all scripts: if any PLAY_SOUND (type 3) opcode references this soundIdx, prompts per-script ('%s skill will no longer use this sound'); any cancel aborts the delete.
  - Walks all scriptItems: for every PLAY_SOUND opcode, sound refs == soundIdx are set to 0 (silence), refs > soundIdx are decremented.
  - Same fixup for the active stage-item or demo-item script if in stage/demo mode.
  - GlobalFree the sound data. memmove shifts slots [soundIdx+1..255] down by one KgtSoundSlotEntry (42 bytes). Clears slot 255.
  - MarkSlotModified + ResetEditorViewState.

0x433de0.name: SwapSoundEntries
0x433de0.repeatable:
Sound-slot CRUD — SWAP entries at idxA and idxB.

Swaps the two 42-byte KgtSoundSlotEntry records via a GlobalAlloc scratch buffer. Then walks all 65536 scriptItems; for each PLAY_SOUND opcode (type 3) calls SwapWordIndices(item, idxA, idxB) to remap the referenced sound index. Same update applied to the active stage or demo slot's script if the editor is in stage/demo mode.

0x434700.name: StopCDAudio
0x434700.repeatable: Stops CD-audio playback: sends MCI_STOP (0x808) then MCI_CLOSE (0x804) to g_cdMciDeviceId and clears g_cdPlaybackActive. Previously mis-named PlayNextCDAudioTrack; renamed StopCDAudio.

0x434760.name: PlayCDAudioTrack
0x434760.repeatable: Opens the CD-audio MCI device and plays track g_cdRequestedTrack. Probes drive letters if the first attempt fails. Uses command codes 0x803=MCI_OPEN, 0x80D=MCI_SET, 0x806=MCI_PLAY (with MCI_NOTIFY=0x0D flags), 0x804=MCI_CLOSE.

0x434890.name: InitializeCDAudio
0x434890.repeatable: Initializes CD audio: stores track/loop params, seeds drive letter 'A' (0x41+0=0x41), calls PlayCDAudioTrack; on failure iterates drive letters probing IsDriveCDROM.

0x434920.name: StopSoundPlayback
0x434920.repeatable: Stops MIDI playback (MCI_STOP + MCI_CLOSE on g_midiMciDeviceId) and clears g_midiPlaybackActive.

0x434960.name: DeleteTempMidiFile
0x434960.repeatable: Deletes %WINDIR%\enterbrain2dfightermak.mid (legacy Fighter Maker-era temp file - the current MIDI uses enterbrain2dkgt2.mid).

0x4349a0.name: StartMidiPlayback
0x4349a0.repeatable: Opens/starts playback of %WINDIR%\enterbrain2dkgt2.mid via MCI 'sequencer'. Despite the name, does not play WAV - only MIDI. Called by PlayMidiFromSoundEntry after it writes the MIDI bytes to that temp file.

0x434ab0.name: PlayMidiFromSoundEntry
0x434ab0.repeatable: Writes sound-entry's MIDI data to %WINDIR%\enterbrain2dkgt2.mid, stops any current playback, then calls StartMidiPlayback (formerly PlaySoundEffect) to play it.

0x434bd0.name: KeyInputCaptureWndProc
0x434bd0.repeatable: KeyInputCaptureWndProc: modal keyboard-capture popup for rebinding shortcuts. WM_PAINT (0xF): draws 'Press a new key or Escape' prompt + current shortcut label. WM_KEYDOWN (0x100): reads key + modifiers (Ctrl/Shift/Alt state via GetAsyncKeyState) into g_keyboardShortcutTable[g_shortcutSlotToRebind], calls UpdateMenuState, DestroyWindow. ESC cancels. Triggered from HandleMenuCommand cases 0xCE4..0xD06 (menu IDs 3300-3334) via CreateKeyInputCaptureWindow. Class name 'KeyInput' registered separately in InitializeMediaPlayback (misnomer).

0x434f50.name: CreateKeyInputCaptureWindow
0x434f50.repeatable: CreateKeyInputCaptureWindow: spawns the 'KeyInput' class popup (0x200 x 64 px, no style) for shortcut-rebind. Called from HandleMenuCommand with g_shortcutSlotToRebind preset to the menu-item ID minus 3300.

0x435770.name: LaunchTestGame
0x435770.repeatable:
Test-play launcher.

1. ValidateGameData(hWnd) — dirty/missing-resource checks.
2. LoadTestPlaySettings — read %app%.kgt2testplay.ini or similar test-play prefs.
3. DialogBoxParam(DIALOG_TESTGAMESETUP, TestGameSettingsDialogProc) — UI to pick output dir, team count, replay mode, etc.
4. ValidatePlayerSettings(hWnd) — ensure characters/stage/demo are valid.
5. CleanupPreviewState, SaveProjectSettings, SaveTestPlaySettings.
6. SaveProjectFile(currentPath, promptForName=0, isTestPlay=1) — exports .kgt + .player + .stage + .demo bundle into g_testPlayOutputDir (this is ExportTestPlayData's parent call).
7. SetCurrentDirectoryA(Directory) (editor install dir).
8. WinExec(cmd, SW_SHOW=5) with:
     g_testPlayLaunchMode==0 : 'KGT2nd_GAME.exe /T'   (normal test play)
     g_testPlayLaunchMode==1 : 'KGT2nd_GAME.exe /F'   (fight-replay mode)
     g_testPlayLaunchMode==2 : 'KGT2nd_GAME.exe'      (no command-line flag)

Returns WinExec handle on success, -1 (from sub-step) on failure.

0x435850.name: ChooseColorForPalette
0x435850.repeatable: ChooseColorForPalette: ChooseColorA wrapper used to edit the global UI-palette slots starting at g_ui_colors[colorSlotIdx] (15-bit RGB555 packed). Decodes RGB555 -> 8-bit-per-channel for the common dialog, then re-quantizes to 5-bit and re-packs after the user confirms.

0x436960.name: CreateAnimationObject
0x436960.repeatable: ENGINE BUG: 'Objects visually delayed by 1 frame when creating/destroying' -- CreateAnimationObject allocates & zeroes a slot in unk_775900[1024] but does NOT run ExecuteAnimationScript on the new object this frame. The object's first [I] keepTime is only evaluated next tick. Similarly destroyed objects are cleared from array but their last-drawn sprite persists one frame. Logic runs at normal cadence but the render snapshot is 1 frame stale. See docs/editor/engine_bugs_mapped.md.

0x43b970.name: SlotListWndProc
0x43b970.repeatable: kgt2nd class index 51 (g_windowClassBuffers[51]) = 0x7D6EB0. Slot list (character/stage/demo sidebar).

0x43cb80.name: TestPlayExportDlgProc
0x43cb80.repeatable: Test-play Export progress dialog. First OK click calls ExportTestPlayData; updates status labels; re-enables OK button. Second OK click closes with result=1.

0x43ccf0.name: TestWritePermission
0x43ccf0.repeatable: Verifies the test-play output dir is writable: copies %WINDIR%\\win.ini to <outDir>\\write-test-delete.txt via SHFileOperationA. Returns 1 on failure (abort export), 0 on success (and deletes the test file).
